Q13 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) are true about lexical analysis in a compiler?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
(a) Token pattern are defined using regular expressions.
(b) Syntactic errors are handled in the syntax analysis phase not by the lexical phase.
(c) Lexer removes white space but macro expansion done by preprocessor.
(d) Lexical analysis turns the character stream into meaning tokens (line = int, a = etc.).

Solution

No optimization in 3-address code.

Q16 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about a basic block in a compiler:
S1 : A basic block contains no conditional or unconditional jump instruction.
S2 : A basic block may have multiple entry points but only one exit point.
S3 : A basic block is a sequence of instruction with only one entry and one exit point.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
S1 : False: Because a basic block can contain jumps. It just can’t have jumps into the middle of it.
S2 : False: A basic block has only one entry and exit point and no branches except at the end.
S3 : True: Standard definition of basic block.

Q22 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• To refer the non-local held in other activation records, access links are used.
• There is a field for local variables in the activation record.
• Top-down parser may stuck in infinite loop while parsing left recursive grammar.
